back_translation: In the eighteenth year of King Jeroboam, Abijah began to reign over Judah.
He reigned three years in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Micaiah daughter of Uriel of Gibeah. And there was war between Abijah and Jeroboam.
Abijah went into battle with an army of valiant warriors, 400,000 chosen men; and Jeroboam drew up against him 800,000 chosen mighty men of valor.
Abijah stood on Mount Zemaraim in the hill country of Ephraim and said, “Hear me, Jeroboam and all Israel!
Do you not know that the LORD, the God of Israel, gave the kingship over Israel to David and his descendants forever by a covenant of salt?
But Jeroboam son of Nebat, servant of Solomon son of David, rose up and rebelled against his master.
And worthless and wicked men gathered to him and resisted Rehoboam son of Solomon when Rehoboam was young and faint-hearted and could not withstand them.

And now you intend to resist the kingdom of the LORD, which is in the hands of David's descendants, because you are a great army and have with you the golden calves that Jeroboam made for you as gods.

Have you not driven out the priests of the LORD, the sons of Aaron, and the Levites, and made priests for yourselves like the peoples of other lands? Whoever comes to consecrate himself with a young bull and seven rams becomes a priest of what are not gods.
But as for us, the LORD is our God, and we have not forsaken Him. The priests who minister to the LORD are sons of Aaron, and the Levites attend to their duties.

Every morning and every evening they burn to the LORD burnt offerings and fragrant incense; they set the bread of the Presence on the clean table, and light the gold lampstand with its lamps every evening. For we keep the charge of the LORD our God, but you have forsaken Him.

Behold, God is with us at our head, and His priests with battle trumpets to sound the alarm against you. O sons of Israel, do not fight against the LORD, the God of your fathers, for you will not succeed.
But Jeroboam had sent an ambush around behind them, so that his army was in front of Judah and the ambush behind them.
When Judah turned, behold, the battle was before and behind them; and they cried out to the LORD, and the priests blew the trumpets.
Then the men of Judah raised a battle cry, and as they shouted, God routed Jeroboam and all Israel before Abijah and Judah.
The sons of Israel fled before Judah, and God gave them into their hand.
Abijah and his people struck them with a great slaughter; 500,000 chosen men of Israel fell slain.
So the sons of Israel were subdued at that time, and the sons of Judah prevailed because they relied on the LORD, the God of their fathers.
Abijah pursued Jeroboam and captured cities from him: Bethel with its villages, Jeshanah with its villages, and Ephron with its villages.
Jeroboam did not regain power in the days of Abijah; and the LORD struck him down, and he died.
But Abijah grew strong; he took fourteen wives and fathered twenty-two sons and sixteen daughters.
Now the rest of the acts of Abijah, his ways and his sayings, are written in the treatise of the prophet Iddo.
Abijah rested with his fathers and was buried in the City of David, and Asa his son reigned in his place. In his days the land was quiet for ten years.
